message:
🌱 Add atlantis: [Bug] unable to unmarshal conftest output or Anyone able to mission (#1164)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "atlantis-4243-bug-unable-to-unmarshal-conftest-output-or-anyone-able-to-approve-",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "atlantis: [Bug] unable to unmarshal conftest output or Anyone able to approve Atlantis policy failures ",
+    "description": "[Bug] unable to unmarshal conftest output or Anyone able to approve Atlantis policy failures . This issue affects 21+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify atlantis troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your atlantis deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review atlantis configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```
### Community Note

* Please vote on this issue by adding a 👍 [reaction](https://blog.github.com/2016-03-10-add-reactions-to-pull-requests-issues-and-comments/) to the original issue to help the community and maintainers prioritize this request."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Bug] unable to unmarshal conftest output or Anyone able to…",
+        "description": "## what

- Propagates policy set names to custom policy sets properly instead of defaulting all custom checks to an undefined set called "Custom"

## why

- Currently, using custom policy tools with Atlantis inadvertently allows any user to successfully execute `atlantis approve_policies` on the
```yaml
container_definition_environment = [
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_CHECKOUT_STRATEGY"
      value = var.checkout_strategy
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ALLOW_COMMANDS"
      value = var.atlantis_allow_commands
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ALLOW_REPO_CONFIG"
      value = var.allow_repo_config
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_RESOURCE_BUCKET_NAME"
      value = data.aws_s3_bucket.atlantis_bucket.id

    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_LOG_LEVEL"
      value = var.atlantis_log_level
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ASSUME_ROLE_NAME"
      value = var.atlantis_assume_role_
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [Bug] unable to unmarshal conftest output or… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "## what

- Propagates policy set names to custom policy sets properly instead of defaulting all custom checks to an undefined set called "Custom"

## why

- Currently, using custom policy tools with Atlantis inadvertently allows any user to successfully execute `atlantis approve_policies` on the policy check
-  Policy set naming is used to map user permissions for who can approve what, and as a",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "container_definition_environment = [
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_CHECKOUT_STRATEGY"
      value = var.checkout_strategy
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ALLOW_COMMANDS"
      value = var.atlantis_allow_commands
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ALLOW_REPO_CONFIG"
      value = var.allow_repo_config
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_RESOURCE_BUCKET_NAME"
      value = data.aws_s3_bucket.atlantis_bucket.id

    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_LOG_LEVEL"
      value = var.atlantis_log_level
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ASSUME_ROLE_NAME"
      value = var.atlantis_assume_role_name
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_PORT"
      value = var.atlantis_port
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_CONFIG"
      value = var.atlantis_config
    },
    {
      name  = "ATLANTIS_ATLA",
+        "repos:
- id: "/.*/"
  branch: "/master/"
  pre_workflow_hooks:
    - description: Pull the Atlantis policies.
  post_workflow_hooks:
    - run: python3 /home/atlantis/review.py 
  allow_custom_workflows: false
  custom_policy_check: true
  policy_check: true
  allowed_overrides: [workflow, apply_requirements, delete_source_branch_on_merge]
  apply_requirements: [approved, mergeable]
  import_requirements: [approved, mergeable]

metrics:
  prometheus:
    endpoint: /metrics

policies:
    conftest_version: 0.45.0
    owners:
      users:
        - user1
        - user2
        - user3
    policy_sets:
        - name: audit_policy
          path: /home/atlantis/policy/security
          source: local
          approve_count: 2
          owners:
            users:
              - securityuser",
+        "version: 3
automerge: true
delete_source_branch_on_merge: true

projects:
- name: test-case/default-workflow
  dir: test-case/default-workflow
  workspace: default
  autoplan:
    when_modified: ["*.tf", "../modules/**.tf", "Terrafile"]
    enabled: true
  workflow: default"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
